story_title: Israeli strikes and Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon
tldr: The dominant headline frame is kinetic SECURITY_DEFENSE: Israeli strikes and Hezbollah vows of retaliation. Religious press and Turkey uniquely foreground attacks on Christian sites (MORALITY), while German and US outlets lead with displacement and civilian experience (QUALITY_OF_LIFE). Indian and opinion outlets foreground Hezbollah disarmament politics.

frames.sub_frame: IDF strikes and Hezbollah retaliation cycle

evidence.quote: 15 killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon
Israel attacks villages north of Litani

frames.sub_frame: Hezbollah disarmament talks and Netanyahu's bind

evidence.quote: weapons not part of negotiations with Israel
Netanyahu has pledged to 'finish the job'
Trump might feel forced to resume war
Churches under attack in southern Lebanon
desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium
Inside the Christian border town that survived by defying

bottom_line: Lebanon headline framing splits on audience: most buckets advertise the kinetic strike-and-retaliation cycle; religious press and Turkey uniquely foreground the destruction of Christian sites, a MORALITY frame absent from all other buckets; German and US outlets lead with human displacement over military action.
